scenario_results.reason: Workforce-structure guidance stayed inside HRIS configuration, effective dating, and rollback planning without widening into payroll or legal advice.
Hire-workflow recommendations improved event and approval quality while preserving downstream ownership boundaries.
Provisioning analysis used official Microsoft and SCIM guidance for mapping and scoping while routing IAM ownership appropriately.
Job-relationship and workflow corrections remained audit-friendly and bounded to HRIS event routing.
Access review identified least-privilege and segregation controls without overclaiming compliance authority.
Release-readiness output tied promotion guidance to vendor release sources, regression focus areas, and rollback steps.
Mass-update guidance emphasized effective-dated validation, sampling, and reversibility rather than risky bulk execution.
Edge-case handling acknowledged concurrent-employment constraints and sync dependencies instead of inventing unsupported behavior.
Payroll-result ownership was rejected clearly with correct routing to a payroll specialty while retaining HRIS event context only.
Custom integration coding was rejected and routed to engineering while preserving platform-side mapping guidance only.
Low-confidence troubleshooting was escalated with explicit evidence requirements instead of speculative root-cause claims.
Fallback handling preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided claims beyond specialist-owned readiness evidence.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #137 requirements, including platform configuration, workflow and integration quality, operations and governance handling, edge-case constraints, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
